titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Margaret A. Farley papers
titlestmt.author: Abi Mason and Elizabeth Peters

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday May 14, 2020 at 22:00

did.abstract: The Margaret Farley Papers include materials relating to Farley's career as a Christian Ethics professor and her research, writings, and course materials relating to subject fields such as sexual ethics, history of christian ethics, medical ethics, and more.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Farley's Papers are organized by Margaret Farley herself, but arranged into four series: Courses (alphabetical), Committee Materials, Writings (Papers and Presentations), and Student Files. The materials are organized into folders the way they were received, and generally arranged topically.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Margaret Farley, born on April 15, 1935, is best known for teaching Christian ethics. She is a Gilbert L. Stark Professor Emerita of Christian Ethics at Yale Divinity School, where she taught from 1971 until 2007. She earned her Bachelors and Masters of Arts degree from the University of Detroit before pursuing her Ph.D. at Yale University. Farley is a member of the Catholic Sisters of Mercy and a previous president of the Catholic Theological Society of America. She was not only one of the first Catholic faculty members at Yale University, but the first woman appointed to the Yale School Board. Her accolades include receiving the John Courtney Murray Award from the Catholic Theological Society of America in 1992, serving as president of the Society of Christian Ethics, and the University of Louisville Grawemeyer Award in Religion in 2008. Though Farley retired in 2007, her research continues.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Margaret Farley papers (RG 29), Special Collections, Yale Divinity School Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Margaret Farley's research interests cover history of theological ethics, feminist ethics, phenomenology and ethics, medical ethics, sexual ethics, ethics and spirituality, and responses to the AIDS pandemic in Africa. These Papers include course materials by session, publications and presentations, and materials from Farley's committee work on stem cell research, in-vitro fertilization, and sex-selection. The course materials for Feminist Theological Ethics, History of Christian Theological Ethics, Sexual Ethics, Environmental Ethics, Medical Ethics, as well as some reading courses on various specific courses. Moreover, her papers include copies of Farley's writings such as selections from Just Love, her contribution to Yale University's sexual harassment policies, and research materials on topics ranging from the Georgetown University homosexual group controversy, pornography, hookup culture, and views on homosexual clergy. The majority of Farley's research materials include opinions from various points of view, and is a general inventory of conversations covering controversial topics over the course of several decades with the inclusion of her writings and interaction with these topics.

corpname.part: United States. National Bioethics Advisory Commission
American College of Obstetricians and Gynecologists

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Preparatory notes for the course and handwritten notes and outlines for the first session.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Articles used as preparation for the course and used as introductory material in the first session.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes readings on women and biblical interpretation, and feminist readings of historical texts including biblical material.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This session explores images of God, theory of 'images', and the relationship between each and human experience as a source for feminist theology and ethics.

did.unittitle: Session 8: The Church and Communities of Faith
Session 12: Spirituality and Justice and Course Conclusion

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes book reserve lists and course reading material.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes timelines for the lives of and general bibliography for St. Augustine, Thomas Aquinas, Martin Luther, and John Calvin including both primary and secondary sources.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Outlines for Sessions 8,9,10,11,12,14,17 on Augustine, Aquinas, Luther, and Calvin

did.unittitle: St. Augustine - On The Notion of Time
St. Augustine to Thomas Aquinas - Transition Lecture Notes
Thomas Aquinas and Human Action Notes
John Calvin - Ethics of Calvinism Notes and Readings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes course wrap-up notes, information on final papers and the final exam, and final exam materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Syllabi included for the following semesters: Fall 1974, Fall 1982, Spring 1986, Fall 1988, Fall 1995, Fall 2000, Fall 2002, and Fall 2005.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A collection of reading lists and book selections selected for the course. Bibliographies vary topically, but some of them pulled from other courses such as a Yale Divinity Spring 1974 course on Prayer: Religious Practice and Religious Experience and a Harvard Divinity School Ethics Colloquium on Normative Theory.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Articles in this file are in order of publication date.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Articles in this file are undated, and therefore in no particular order.

did.unittitle: Course Reading Materials on Women and Feminism
Session 2: Meanings of Spirituality in Human History

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This file includes handwritten notes, articles, etc.

did.unittitle: Session 4: Notes on Virtue and Moral Development

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This file includes outlines, annotations, and notes written by Margaret Farley in preparation for this session.

did.unittitle: Session 4: Readings on Virtue and Moral Development

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This file includes articles and readings used in preparation for and assigned for this session.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This file includes both articles and notes pertaining to only the first half of this session, the portion on Mysticism. Some outlines are labeled Session 4, but its placement in the session 5 folder and content allow it to remain.

did.unittitle: Session 5: Notes on Moral and Spiritual Call, Scripture, Prayer

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This file includes notes and handouts pertaining to the second half of this session, the portion on moral and spiritual call, scripture, and prayer.

did.unittitle: Session 5: Readings on Moral and Spiritual Call, Scripture, Prayer

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This file includes articles and reading material relevant for the second half of this session, the portion on moral and spiritual call, scripture, and prayer.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Some outlines say Session 8, but before 2005 all this content was part of session 9, therefore materials notated this way remain in the session 9 file with other related content.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes article published in the Yale Bulletin about the course entitled, "Project Boosts Interdisciplinary Debate about Bioethical Issues"

did.unittitle: Session 10: Potential for Harm and Violence
Sessions 11 and 12: Constructing a Sexual Ethic

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: On sexual violence, sexual harassment, pornography, abortion, homosexuality and lesbianism, and general sexual ethics both generally and in relation to the church and religion

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This file includes mainly handwritten notes

did.unittitle: Research on Gender Difference and Roles

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Articles include writings on gender difference in behavior, lesbianism, and feminist perspectives

did.unittitle: Research on Pornography and Sex Work

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a Spring 1994 pornography specific sexual ethics syllabus offered at an unknown institution, and likely taught by someone other than Margaret Farley. Readings predominantly feature female and feminist point of views on the sex industry, including prostitution and pornography. Newspaper and magazine clippings are international, including perspectives from Japan and Thailand. Book lists specific to these subjects are also in this file. Correspondence in regard to these subjects are included.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes an email including a July/August 2008 newsletter published online by The Center for Lesbian and Gay Studies in Religion and Ministry (CLGS) out of Pacific School and Religion in Berkley, CA.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Historical information, modern articles, and personal notes by Margaret Farley included

did.unittitle: Research on Domestic and Sexual Violence

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials include resource guides for domestic and sexual violence, book lists on domestic violence, and articles and notes about both domestic and sexual violence. Be aware that the content in this file addresses incest, sexual abuse, rape as a weapon of war, rape in the form of "military comfort women," and female circumcision (also known as Female Genital Mutilation, FGM).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This file includes bibliographies, case studies, and discussion questions.

did.unittitle: Research on Social Views of Homosexuality

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Writings concerning social, philosophical, and non-scientific academic opinions on homosexuality and lesbianism. Religion and spirituality are relevant topics in many of these articles.

did.unittitle: Research on Homosexuality and the Clergy

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Articles concerning ordination of homosexual persons with many personal testimonials and church responses and opinions.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: In the 1970's, a Catholic bishop named John McNeill came out as gay. His story, and his position within the church, sparked debate and conflict that resulted in Vatican involvement and international outcry. Newspapers and articles written about and by John McNeill concerning his position in the church and the position of the church regarding McNeill are included.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Articles and newspaper clippings concerning political matters and homosexuality including on civil rights, marriage, and military involvement.

did.unittitle: Research Case: Georgetown Ban on Homosexual Groups

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Articles and opinions related to the Gay Rights Coalition student group vs. Georgetown University lawsuit. In the early 1980's, Georgetown University in conjunction with the Roman Catholic Church banned two student groups related to homosexuality. Following the event, the issue resulted in 8 years of lawsuits and made national news. In 1987, the groups were granted equal treatment but not official recognition by the university allowing their use of facilities and granted financing like other student groups. A letter from alumni to Georgetown from April of 1988 is the last article included in this file.

did.unittitle: Bibliographies and Case Studies on Sexual Harassment

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Farley did research and work on sexual harassment policy for Yale University and the topic blends into her sexual ethics course.

did.unittitle: Law and Opinion on Sexual Harassment
Definitions and Policy Creation Regarding Sexual Harassment
Research on Sexual Harassment in Medicine
Research on Sexual Harassment in the Church
Christian Perspective on Human Sexuality Course: Syllabi
Environmental Ethics: Course Information and Materials
Environmental Ethics: Course Bibliographies and Readings
Ethics and Family Life Course: Syllabi
Ethics and Family Life Course: Notes
Ethics and Family Life Course: Bibliographies and Readings
Natural Law and Christian Ethics Reading Course

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This file includes syllabi and a student paper written by Tracy G. Gipson.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This file includes syllabi, reading list, notes, and student papers by James (Jim) Tuite (multiple), Susan J. McCone, J. Patout Burns, S.J., and William Danaher.

did.unittitle: Women's Movement Course: Syllabi and Course Materials
Women's Movement Course: Course Bibliographies and Readings
Women's Movement Course: Session 4 Issues of Sex and Sexuality
Women's Movement Course: Session 5 Lesbian Relations
Women's Movement Course: Sessions 6 & 7 Feminist Ethics

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes article published in the Yale Bulletin about the course entitled, "Project Boosts Interdisciplinary Debate about Bioethical Issues" (2000); Outline and notes regarding the Yale Bioethics Project (2003); Materials from 2005 project "Ethics, Bioethics and Medical Ethics;" and related course materials associated with the interdisciplinary collaborative project.

did.unittitle: ACOG Ethical Committee: Ethical Dimensions of Informed Consent Opinion

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: American College of Obstetricians and Gynecologists (ACOG) Committee on Ethics Opinion Publication Number 108. Several drafts from October 1991 (originally 'Feminist Dimensions of Informed Consent' and final publication, 'Ethical Dimensions of Informed Consent'.

did.unittitle: National Bioethics Advisory Commission (NBAC) on Stem Cell Research

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes agenda and correspondence in regards to the 30th NBAC meeting. Also in this file are draft copies and the final version of the NBAC Ethical Issues In Human Stem Cell Research Religious Perspectives Volume III (Rockland, MD: June 2000). Other testimonies and articles included, as well as notes by Margaret Farley in reference to her testimonial contribution. Although the materials in this file range from early 1999 to June 2000, the National Bioethics Advisory Commission held a meeting on May 7, 1999 and several of the talks presented on this day are included. Later materials in this file are in regard to some of the subjects addressed at this meeting, and used to draft the June 2000 NBAC journal publication.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: American College of Obstetricians and Gynecologists (ACOG) Committee on Ethics Opinion Publication Number 138 (04/1994), "Pre-embryo Research: History, Scientific Background, and Ethical Considerations," authored by Margaret Farley on behalf of the committee. Other materials include correspondence regarding the committee meeting and subject matter, as well as numerous articles and published opinions. The materials also include articles and papers written about the subject after the April 1994 committee opinion was published.

did.unittitle: ACOG and ASRM Ethical Committees: Ethics of Sex Selection

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: American College of Obstetricians and Gynecologists (ACOG) Committee on Ethics Opinion Publication Number 177 (11/1996), "Sex Selection." Other materials include articles, opinions, newspaper selection, and correspondence relevant to the topic.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: ACOG Committee on Ethics opinion Number 55 (10/1987), "Patient Choice: Maternal-Fetal Conflict," and draft of opinion entitled, "Counseling and Consent for Testing: Physicians' Responsibilities." Variety materials including articles, opinions, and correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Research materials, current event articles, and book reviews spanning a decade concerning the subjects of in-vitro fertilization, sperm donation, multiple fetuses, and the legal rights of surrogates and sperm/egg donors.

did.unittitle: Yale Ethics of Stem Cell Research Working Group: Stem-Cell Research

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Communication and research materials for the Yale Ethics Committee on stem cell research

did.unittitle: Yale New Haven Hospital Bioethics Committee

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mission statement and Administrative Policy and Procedure Manual (two versions) of the Bioethics Committee of Yale New Haven Hospital written by Margaret Farley

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This file includes papers given and research in reference to this meeting of the National Bioethics Advisory Commission regarding the theme of Stem Cell Research and Therapy: A Judeo-Biblical Perspective. These papers include: The Ethics of the Eighth Day: Jewish Bioethics and Genetic Medicine by Laurie Zoloth, Stem Cell Research by Rabbi Elliot N. Dorff, Remarks on Human Embryonic Stem Cell Research by Ronald Cole-Turner, An Eastern Orthodox View of Embryonic Stem Cell Research by Rev. Demetrius Demopoulos, and Remarks on Human Embryonic Stem-Cell Research by Gilbert Meilaender.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This committee opinion addresses embryonic stem cells for stem cell research and the ethics of donating embryonic stem cells. An undated draft of this opinion is included, alongside correspondence and other research materials.

did.unittitle: Partnership in Hope: Gender, Faith, and Responses to HIV/AIDS in Africa

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This paper was written as a reflection of an initiative for HIV/AIDS research begun at summit in 2000.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Drafts of Margaret Farley's article which was originally published in Volume 1 of Handbook of Stem Cells published by Academic Press (pp. 765-773) (copy included) and adapted for 'Essentials of Stem Biology,' which was published first in 2005, and then revised and republished in 2009 (copy included). Correspondence regarding this publication is included.

did.unittitle: Cloning for Research: Ethical and Religious Concerns

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two drafts of 'Cloning for Research: Ethical and Religious Concerns' with annotations.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Draft of paper entitled, 'Tubal Ligation: An Intrinsic Evil?' as well as a copy of the October 1980 report on 'Tubal Ligation in Hospitals Sponsored by the Sisters of Mercy of the Union.'

did.unittitle: Roman Catholic Perspectives on Medical Futility

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A never-published paper co-written by Jennifer Beste and Margaret A. Farley, including a draft with annotations.

did.unittitle: Assisted Death: Morality and Public Policy

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a final and a draft copy with annotations

did.unittitle: Roman Catholic Views on Research Involving Human Embryonic Cells

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Draft of paper first published in June 2000 and re-published in April of 2001 as chapter 10 (pp.113-118) in "The Human Embryonic Stem Cell Debate: Science, Ethics and Public Policy," edited by Holland, Lebacquz and Zoloth. Also included is all correspondence associated with the editing and publication of this chapter contribution.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bibliography compiled on the date noted, and therefore only includes materials for research that were published before that date.

did.unittitle: The Ethics of Stem Cell Research (Gene Outka)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Speech, Drafted paper, and published version of Gene Outka's "The Ethics of Stem Cell Research." Margaret Farley used these articles as research in writing "Roman Catholic Views on Research Involving Human Embryonic Stem Cells."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Farley's handwritten notes used while writing "Roman Catholic Views on Research Involving Human Embryonic Stem Cells" and a filled out mailing label

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Scientific, political, and ethical writings on stem cell research. This includes articles emailed to Margaret Farley with attached materials. All materials used as research in writing "Roman Catholic Views on Research Involving Human Embryonic Stem Cells."

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: In order of publication, with undated at the end of the file.

did.unittitle: Religious Opinions on Stem Cell Research

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These materials all evaluate stem cell research in light of religious concerns, primarily reflective of Catholic perspectives. This includes articles emailed to Margaret Farley with attached materials. Materials used as research in writing "Roman Catholic Views on Research Involving Human Embryonic Stem Cells" and "Stem Cell Research: Religious Considerations."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Margaret Farley's contribution entitled 'Sexual Ethics' in Encyclopedia of Bioethics, Volume 4, edited by Warren T. Reich`. This section also includes ethics regarding homosexuality, and was used in conjunction with her sexual ethics course.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence with Crossroads/Continuum publishing and Farley in regard to the book. Outlines and draft contents of book included.

did.unittitle: Just Love , Chapter 4: Body-- Sexuality and Its Meaning

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes copies of Farley's "Transcendent Embodiment" as preliminary work regarding the same topic.

did.unittitle: Just Love , Chapter 6: Framework For a Sexual Ethic

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This is a full draft of chapter 6 from Just Love, and includes notes and research materials for the chapter. This also has a floppy disk.

did.unittitle: The Church and The Family: An Ethical Task

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two copies of the published version in Horizon 10/1 (1983), pgs. 50-71, one draft from 1983 for Horizon, and an earlier draft from 1983.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Originally entitled "Catholic Social Teaching and Family" and then "Ideology of Family," these drafts were published in The New Dictionary of Catholic Social Thought from Liturgical Press, Ed. Judith A. Dwyer, under the title of "Family."

did.unittitle: Ethical Issues Surrounding Human Stem Cell Research

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Address given at the New Haven International Festival of Arts and Ideas.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials regarding CT Law Revision Commission hearing about surrogacy. Margaret Farley gave a speech during this hearing on 10/25/1999. Other materials were included in this hearing. Materials also includes are meeting agendas, committee lists, correspondence (including with Margaret Farley), and a completed copy of Assisted Reproductive Technologies Monograph and Art Model Act (Published by the Family Law Section of the American Bar Association).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Offered as the Earl Lecture at the Pacific School of Religion. Two incomplete drafts with notes and handwritten additions and annotations.

did.unittitle: Methodology and Ethics: Relevance for the Family

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lecture given at Yale University in 1980.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This presentation is a response to Mary Rose D'Angelo's paper on "Early Christian Sexual Politics and Roman Imperial Family Values: Rereading Christ and Culture."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Research materials including notes for a lecture Margaret Farley gave at Fairfield University

did.unittitle: Yale University Women's Organization Lecture Series

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes and Outline for 6 part lecture series for Yale University Women's Organization

did.unittitle: Dobihal Lecture: "Beyond Autonomy VS. Beneficence: Compassionate Respect"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Autonomy: The Principle of Self-Determination and Its Impact In Acute/Long Term Care Settings." Seminar sponsored by The Connecticut Hospital Association Health Care Ethics Resource Center, The CT Association of Not-for-Profit Providers for the Aging, and The Institute for Long Term Care Policy.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Palliative Care: Recovering the Best of the Catholic Tradition,' speech presented in St. Louis at The Supportive Care Coalition's annual congress.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Farley's University work on Thomas Aquinas, University of Detroit undergrad notebook on History of Philosophy, and undergraduate thesis "St. Thomas: Divine Causality and Human Freedom"
